denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 05/10/2026
Cigna Open Access Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100847333          Group #: GRP-1107
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-118680
Claim #: CLM-2026-193672
Date of Service (requested): 04/20/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Harborview Clinical Partners

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 04/22/2026 for Continuous glucose monitor (Dexcom G7) related to Type 2 diabetes mellitus with labile glucose (E11.65). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Continuous glucose monitor (Dexcom G7)
Diagnosis (as submitted): Type 2 diabetes mellitus with labile glucose (E11.65)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: Coverage Policy 127 under Cigna Medical Coverage Policy (CPG/MM_ series) and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG)

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in Coverage Policy 127 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Cigna medical policy requires documented failure of prerequisite therapies before Continuous glucose monitor (Dexcom G7). Submitted records do not establish failure of required lower-cost options for Type 2 diabetes mellitus with labile glucose (E11.65).

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with step therapy missing. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a Cigna medical director regarding this determination before or during the appeal process., they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Many members use this information when preparing an appeal. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination through our internal appeals process. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100847333) and reference UM-2026-118680. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Cigna Health Care Management
Medical Review Unit
clinical_context: The 34-year-old member has Type 2 diabetes mellitus with labile glucose (E11.65). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Continuous glucose monitor (Dexcom G7) recommended as the next appropriate step. Basal-bolus regimen with nocturnal hypoglycemia to 48 mg/dL on fingersticks. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that step therapy missing bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.
